reasoning: The informal statement says a closed subspace of a normal space is normal. The Lean statement takes a topological space X with `NormalSpace X`, a subset A with `IsClosed A`, and concludes `NormalSpace {x // x ∈ A}`. The subtype `{x // x ∈ A}` automatically carries the subspace topology in Mathlib, so `NormalSpace` on this subtype exactly means the closed subspace A (with the subspace topology) is normal. The hypotheses (normal ambient space, closed subset) and conclusion (subspace is normal) match the informal claim precisely, with no added or omitted assumptions.
